description_long: This object is a detailed model of a fiddler crab, characterized by its asymmetrical claws, with one claw being significantly larger than the other. The carapace is a mottled brown and olive color, with a rough, textured surface, while the legs are slender and jointed, showing a gradient from dark brown to lighter hues. The large claw is pale pinkish-white with a granular texture, contrasting with the smaller, darker claw. Two long, thin eye stalks protrude from the top of the carapace, giving the crab a distinctive appearance. The underside and joints of the legs display a lighter, almost translucent coloration. The overall form is compact, with the body and appendages arranged in a lifelike, naturalistic pose.
description: A lifelike fiddler crab with a large pale claw, mottled brown carapace, and slender jointed legs. Prominent eye stalks and detailed textures enhance its realism.
description_view_0: Side view highlighting the oversized pale claw and the crab's compact body, with both eye stalks visible.
description_view_1: Front-angled view emphasizing the granular texture of the large claw and the arrangement of the legs.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the dark, mottled carapace and the symmetrical arrangement of the walking legs.
description_view_3: Oblique rear-side view displaying the curvature of the large claw and the segmentation of the legs.
